questions.question: What is the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning?
questions.answer: Supervised: labeled training data, predicts output from input (classification, regression). Unsupervised: unlabeled data, finds patterns (clustering, dimensionality reduction). Semi-supervised combines both.
questions.explanation: In supervised learning, the model learns a mapping from inputs to outputs using labeled examples. Unsupervised learning discovers hidden structure without guidance, useful for exploration and preprocessing.
